ud4.4:1.1:
1
Thus I heard:
At one time the Gracious One was dwelling near Rājagaha, in Bamboo Wood, at the Squirrels’ Feeding Place. Then at that time venerable Sāriputta and venerable Mahāmoggallāna were living at the Pigeon Vale. Then at that time venerable Sāriputta, on a moonlight night, with his hair freshly shaved off, was sitting in the open air, having attained a certain concentration.
ud4.4:1.2:
2
Then at that time two yakkha friends were going from the Northern region to the Southern region, with some business or other. Those yakkhas saw venerable Sāriputta, on
moonlight night, with his hair freshly shaved off, sat in the open air.
ud4.4:1.3:
And after seeing
, one of those yakkhas said this to the second yakkha: "It occurs to me, friend, to give a blow on this ascetic's head."
ud4.4:1.4:
When that was said, that yakkha said this to the
yakkha: "Enough, friend, don’t strike the ascetic. Eminent is the ascetic, friend, of great power, of great majesty."
ud4.4:1.5:
3
For a second time that yakkha said this to the
yakkha: "It occurs to me, friend, to give a blow on this ascetic's head."
ud4.4:1.6:
For a second time that yakkha said this to the
yakkha: "Enough, friend, don’t strike the ascetic. Eminent is the ascetic, friend, of great power, of great majesty."
ud4.4:1.7:
For a third time that yakkha said this to the
yakkha: "It occurs to me, friend, to give a blow on this ascetic's head."
ud4.4:1.8:
For a third time that yakkha said this to the
yakkha: "Enough, friend, don’t strike the ascetic. Eminent is the ascetic, friend, of great power, of great majesty."
ud4.4:1.9:
4
Then the yakkha taking no notice of that
yakkha, gave a blow on the elder venerable Sāriputta's head: it was such a great blow that with that blow a seven, or seven and a half cubit Nāga
might have been felled, or a great mountain top might have been burst open.
ud4.4:1.10:
Then that yakkha
: "I'm burning, I'm burning" right there
fell into the Great Hell.
ud4.4:1.11:
5
Venerable Mahāmoggallāna saw with his divine-eye, which is purified, and surpasses that of
men, that yakkha giving a blow on venerable Sāriputta's head. And after seeing
, he went to venerable Sāriputta, and after going, he said this to venerable Sāriputta: "Can you bear up, venerable friend? Can you carry on? Do you have any pain?"
ud4.4:1.12: "I can bear up, friend Moggallāna; I can carry on, friend Moggallāna, but I have a little pain on my head."
ud4.4:1.13:
6
"Wonderful, friend Sāriputta, marvellous, friend Sāriputta, such is the venerable Sāriputta's great power and great majesty. Here, friend Sāriputta, a certain yakkha gave a blow on your head: it was such a great blow, that with that blow a seven, or seven and a half cubit Nāga
might have been felled, or a great mountain top might have been burst open. But then venerable Sāriputta said this: ‘I can bear up, friend Moggallāna; I can carry on, friend Moggallāna, but I have a little pain on my head.’ "
ud4.4:1.14:
7
"Wonderful, friend Moggallāna, marvellous, friend Moggallāna, such is the venerable Mahāmoggallāna's great power and great majesty, in as much as he can even see a yakkha, whereas we at present do not even see a mud-demon!"
ud4.4:1.15:
8
The Gracious One heard with his divine ear-element, which is purified, and surpasses that of
men, the fitting talk and conversation of these two Great Nāgas.
ud4.4:1.16:
9
Then the Gracious One, having understood the significance of it on that occasion uttered this exalted utterance:
ud4.4:1.17:
10
"For he whose mind is like a rock, steady, and not wavering,
Unexcited by what is exciting, not agitated by what is agitating,
For he whose mind is developed thus
from where will pain come upon him?"
